content.rendered:
Jim van der Zee is just a few days shy of his 26th birthday, but his career is off to a great start.

In 2018, the Dutch singer-songwriter was the winning contestant on The Voice of Holland, and with the record deal he won from that competition, he released his debut album, Where I Come From.

Where I Come From  is a cover album, featuring ten songs by van der Zee’s musical heroes, including a wonderful cover of Bruce Springsteen’s “Highway 29.”

Not only are van der Zee’s vocals terrific (we’d expect as much given his performance on The Voice), but the arrangement is superb as well. “Highway 29” is a quiet, contemplative song; van der Zee preserves its spirit while adorning it with restrained orchestration that never overpowers the lyrics.

But as is so often the case with the original artist, it’s when van der Zee performs the song in concert that he truly captivates. In this 2019 performance, Jim is accompanied only by piano and his own guitar, and it’s even better than his studio version.

© January 12, 2021

excerpt.rendered: Dutch singer-songwriter catapulted his career into motion by covering one of his musical hero’s best-known songs and one of his least-known.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Darlene Love: Just Another Lonely Mile
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Darlene Love's debut album featured this Springsteen-authored, Van Zandt-produced, Spectorian gem.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Gregory Alan Isakov and Patty Griffin: Highway 29
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Patty Griffin and Gregory Alan Isakov go all-Bruce in the encores of their current tour. Here's one of my favorites from the the other night in Portland.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Confessin’ the Blues
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Listen to Bruce rejoin his former band to take lead vocals on Walter Brown's blues standard, "Confessin' the Blues."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Tupelo Honey
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: During sound checks on the Tunnel of Love Tour, Bruce occasionally covered one of Van Morrison's loveliest songs.

jetpack-related-posts.title: London Calling
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce led an all-star line-up at the 2003 Grammy Awards to pay tribute to the late, great Joe Strummer.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Jump
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Twice only: Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band cover one of the greatest rock anthems of the 1980s.
